title: Transoceanic Empires: Empires and Resistance
description: This exercise will cover a discussion of the establishment and maintenance of maritime empires from 1450 to 1750 CE. This discussion will also cover the themes of GOV, ECN, SIO, and CDI. The items in this exercise will be grouped according to theme and will be scaled with diverse geographic examples. Each of these themes will have a type and each type will scale 2 items, for a total of 12.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

>Dated the 31st December, in the 43rd year of Her Reign. Anno Domini, 1600.
>
>ELIZABETH, by the Grace of God, Queen of England, France, and Ireland, Defender of the Faith, &c.…our well-beloved Subjects,...have of our certain knowledge been Petitioners unto us for our Royal Assent and Licence to be granted unto them, that they, at their own Adventures, Costs, and Charges, as well for the Honour of this our Realm of England, as for the Increase of our Navigation, and Advancement of Trade of Merchandize…
>
>Things whatever, which so shall be brought into this Realm of England…as also the Ship and Ships with the Furniture thereof, wherein such Goods, Merchandizes, or Things shall be brought; the One Half of all the said Forfeitures to be to us, our Heirs and Successors, and the other Half of all and every the said Forfeitures, we do,... grant unto The said Governor and Company of Merchants of London, Trading into the East-Indies…

Source: Charter granted by Queen Elizabeth to the East India Company (1600), Parliament of England. Public domain. 

**The events described in the excerpt above are best understood in the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of mercantilism
the intensification of the transatlantic slave trade
the implementation of socialism
the decline in trade in the Indian Ocean

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

>Dated the 31st December, in the 43rd year of Her Reign. Anno Domini, 1600.
>
>ELIZABETH, by the Grace of God, Queen of England, France, and Ireland, Defender of the Faith, &c.…our well-beloved Subjects,...have of our certain knowledge been Petitioners unto us for our Royal Assent and Licence to be granted unto them, that they, at their own Adventures, Costs, and Charges, as well for the Honour of this our Realm of England, as for the Increase of our Navigation, and Advancement of Trade of Merchandize…
>
>Things whatever, which so shall be brought into this Realm of England…as also the Ship and Ships with the Furniture thereof, wherein such Goods, Merchandizes, or Things shall be brought; the One Half of all the said Forfeitures to be to us, our Heirs and Successors, and the other Half of all and every the said Forfeitures, we do,... grant unto The said Governor and Company of Merchants of London, Trading into the East-Indies…

Source: Charter granted by Queen Elizabeth to the East India Company (1600), Parliament of England. Public domain. 

**Developments such as the one depicted in the excerpt above most directly contributed to which of the following from 1450 to 1750 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Joint-stock companies facilitated imperialism, supporting colonial administration around the world.
Joint-stock companies led to migration routes, allowing immigrants in new areas to eventually lead independence movements.
Joint-stock companies decreased imports, incentivizing industrialization to fill the vacuum of manufactured goods.
Joint-stock companies developed socialism, demonstrating the need for higher government intervention in individual action.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an impact of the expansion of maritime trading networks on the Asante Kingdom from 1450 to 1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Its role in trade networks with Europeans fostered growth.
Its involvement in trade networks in the Indian Ocean created economic expansion.
Its refusal to trade with Europeans allowed it to become a powerful empire.
Its lack of natural resources eventually hindered its trade relationship with Europe.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an example of a continuity in maritime trading networks from 1450 to 1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: While the arrival of Europeans brought some change, Indian Ocean trade continued to flourish for Asian merchants.
The presence of Europeans in the Indian Ocean led to its maritime dominance, which continued European control of trade over land and sea.
While the increase in trade across oceans connected land empires, European maritime empires did not interact with Asian merchants in the Indian Ocean.
The arrival of Europeans destroyed established maritime trade networks in the Indian Ocean, but Asian merchants continued to gain wealth in trade over land.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.** 

[[☃ image 1]]

**The image above is best understood in the context of which of the following broader processes in the period of 1450–1750 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Picture of both sides of a silver coin.
options.caption: Silver Peso of Philip V, King of Spain. Public domain.

choices.content: The global flow of silver from the Spanish colonies in the Americas was used to purchase Asian goods to satisfy Chinese demand.
The global flow of silver from the Spanish colonies in the Americas led to the world’s first global currency.
The discovery of silver in the Spanish Philippines led to an increase in European trade in the Indian Ocean.
The newly discovered silver was traded for industrial-made textiles that were manufactured in Europe.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following most directly contributed to the rise of a capitalist economy in Europe between the years 1450 and 1750 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: credit, joint-stock ventures, and the production of goods
credit, mercantile policies, and the availability of raw materials in Europe
mercantile policies, colonization, and the global silver trade
joint-stock ventures, a global currency, and the Columbian Exchange

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

>Oh! the loathsomeness and filth of that horrible [ship] will never be effaced from my memory... My heart even at this day, sickens at the thought of it. . .The only food we had during the voyage was corn soaked and boiled. I cannot tell how long we were thus confined, but it seemed a very long while. We suffered very much for want of water, but was denied all we needed. A pint a day was all that was allowed, and no more; and a great many slaves died upon the passage. . .

Source: Baquaqua, Mahommah G. Biography of Mahommah G. Baquaqua, a Native of Zoogoo, in the Interior of Africa. OER.

**The excerpt above describes the circumstances that enslaved persons were forced to endure as they crossed the Atlantic Ocean. Which of the following best describes a cause of the conditions described in the excerpt from 1450 to 1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rise of the plantation economy in the Americas created an increased demand for labor.
The discovery of silver in the Americas created an increased demand for labor.
The demand for silk in Europe created an increased demand for labor.
The increase in the export of wool from Europe created an increased demand for labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

>Oh! the loathsomeness and filth of that horrible [ship] will never be effaced from my memory... My heart even at this day, sickens at the thought of it. . .The only food we had during the voyage was corn soaked and boiled. I cannot tell how long we were thus confined, but it seemed a very long while. We suffered very much for want of water, but was denied all we needed. A pint a day was all that was allowed, and no more; and a great many slaves died upon the passage. . .

Source: Baquaqua, Mahommah G. Biography of Mahommah G. Baquaqua, a Native of Zoogoo, in the Interior of Africa. OER.

**Developments such as the one depicted in the excerpt above most directly contributed to which of the following from 1450 to 1750 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the mixing of African, European, and American people and cultures
the arrival of diseases in the Americas
an increase in the number of horses in central America
a decrease in the power of the Asante Kingdom

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

>Oh! the loathsomeness and filth of that horrible [ship] will never be effaced from my memory... My heart even at this day, sickens at the thought of it. . .The only food we had during the voyage was corn soaked and boiled. I cannot tell how long we were thus confined, but it seemed a very long while. We suffered very much for want of water, but was denied all we needed. A pint a day was all that was allowed, and no more; and a great many slaves died upon the passage. . .

Source: Baquaqua, Mahommah G. Biography of Mahommah G. Baquaqua, a Native of Zoogoo, in the Interior of Africa. OER.

**The events described in the excerpt are best understood in the context of which of the following broader processes from 1450 to 1750 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The European demand for labor, as well as its economic might, intensified the Transatlantic Slave Trade.
European demand for raw materials, as well as its maritime dominance, led to Europeans settlers in the interior of Africa.
Native Africans, like the Asante, fueled the slave trade by providing an endless supply of human beings.
Native Africans, once they arrived in the Americas, were taught new agricultural skills and able to gain their freedom after a certain number of years.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following developments could historians use as an example of the cultural impact of European activity in the Indian Ocean trade network from 1450 to 1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Europeans new to the Indian Ocean married locally and blended their languages and religions.
Europeans new to the Indian Ocean converted large portions of the population to Catholicism.
Europeans carried cultural traditions from the Americas to port cities in the Indian Ocean.
European languages became the dominant form of communication in port cities in the Indian Ocean.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the role diaspora communities played in the Indian Ocean trade network from 1450 to 1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They connected large communities through language and common customs.
They dominated the trade in the Indian Ocean until the arrival of Europeans.
They were not trusted by many locals, and therefore moved from port city to port city, carrying new customs.
They hindered trade, leaving the Indian Ocean vulnerable to European dominance.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a religious impact of trade to the Americas from 1450–1750 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of syncretic belief systems
the rejection of monotheism
the acceptance of indigenous American pagan religions in Europe
the decline of Catholicism in Latin America
